detaileddescription.para: This page provides detailed documentation for the various particle classes used in the gnn_event. These classes are used to represent the input nodes to the GNN and the reconstructed particles.

sect1.title: GNN Particle
sect1.para: The particle_gnn class represents a single node in the input graph for the GNN.

listitem.para: particle_gnn(): Initializes the particle type to "gnn-particle" and defines the data leaves to be read from the input tree.
particle_template* clone(): Creates a new instance of the particle_gnn particle.
void build(std::map<std::string, particle_template*>* prt, element_t* el): Populates the particle_gnn particle's properties from the input data.
lep: A boolean flag indicating if the particle is a lepton.

sect1.title: Top
sect1.para: The top class represents a reconstructed top quark. It inherits from particle_template.

listitem.para: top(): Initializes the particle type to "top".
av_score: The average PageRank score of the nodes in the cluster that formed this top quark.
n_leps: The number of leptons in the cluster.
n_nodes: The number of nodes in the cluster.

sect1.title: Z'
sect1.para: The zprime class represents a reconstructed Z' boson. It inherits from particle_template.

listitem.para: zprime(): Initializes the particle type to "zprime".
av_score: The average PageRank score of the nodes in the cluster that formed this Z' boson.
n_leps: The number of leptons in the cluster.
n_nodes: The number of nodes in the cluster.
